title: Bring Back The One
description: A mod that brings back the "1." in front of the minecraft version
body:
# BringBackTheOne

**BringBackTheOne** is a simple client-side mod that restores the classic "1." prefix to Minecraft version numbers.

## The Problem
With recent snapshots (starting from 26.1), the version numbering scheme dropped the leading "1.", resulting in versions like `26.1`. For many veterans, this just feels... wrong.

## The Solution
This mod seamlessly injects "1." back where it belongs across the entire game interface.

### Features
*   **Main Menu**: Restores the version in the bottom-left corner (e.g., `Minecraft 1.26.1`).
*   **F3 Debug Screen**: Corrections the version line in the debug overlay.
*   **World Selection**: Fixes the version displayed in world descriptions.
*   **Window Title**: Updates the game window title to match the classic format.

## Compatibility
*   **Fabric** & **NeoForge** supported.
*   Client-side only (not needed on servers).

## License
MIT License - strict, open, and free to use in any modpack.

## Known Issues
*   The version number on the screen displayed when opening a world from an older version is not updated.
